story: We pried open a shuttered church. In the front pew sat Agnes, frail and dignified, Bible in her arms. I told my crew, “Have the site ready tomorrow—give two graceful old ladies one more day.” I drove her through old haunts, past her house, then to St. Andrew’s; attendants said they’d expected her hours ago. She’d never returned her church keys; slipping one into her sleeve, she promised herself, At dawn I will go—one last time. The doors hushed behind her. That night my phone buzzed: the crew confirming a 6 a.m. start inside the nave.
implausible_knowledge: slipping one into her sleeve, she promised herself, At dawn I will go—one last time.
question: That night, when the crew texts to confirm a 6 a.m. start inside the nave, what will the narrator instruct them to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Delay the start until late morning so Agnes can have a final dawn visit.
answer_without_knowledge: Approve the 6 a.m. start as planned.
